audioProcessing.title: Speech Processing
audioProcessing.description: Configure voice activity detection and speech artifact filtering.
audioProcessing.sonioxRealtimeBypassWarning: Soniox realtime mode ignores Filter Silence and Voice Detection Sensitivity.
audioProcessing.fillerFilterDescription: Automatically remove 'uh', 'um', 'hmm' and similar filler words from transcriptions.
audioProcessing.fillerHelpTitle: Tell me more about filler word removal
audioProcessing.fillerExplanation: This feature automatically removes common filler words and speech artifacts from your transcriptions:
audioProcessing.howItWorksIntro: The filter applies several regex patterns in sequence:
audioProcessing.regexFillers: Removes filler words with word boundaries
audioProcessing.regexStutters: Collapses repeated 1-2 letter words (I I I I → I)
audioProcessing.languageWarning: This feature is optimized for English. If you experience issues with other languages (missing words, incorrect filtering), try disabling this option.
audioProcessing.fillerNote: This is especially useful with AI models like Parakeet that may produce filler words or stuttering artifacts. Most professional dictation systems include similar filtering.
audioProcessing.zeroWidthFilterDescription: Remove invisible Unicode characters from LLM output (post-processing and AI Replace).
audioProcessing.zeroWidthHelpTitle: Tell me more about invisible character removal
audioProcessing.zeroWidthExplanation: Some LLM providers (notably Qwen) insert invisible Unicode characters into their responses. These are zero-width characters that you can't see, but they can cause issues when pasted into other applications.
audioProcessing.zeroWidthBOM: Byte Order Mark / Zero-Width No-Break Space (U+FEFF)
audioProcessing.zeroWidthIssues: These invisible characters can cause unexpected behavior: broken text searches, incorrect string lengths, copy-paste issues, and invisible formatting problems in documents.
audioProcessing.zeroWidthNote: This filter applies to all LLM output — both post-processing and AI Replace. It's safe to leave enabled for all providers.
audioProcessing.microphoneInputBoostWarning: Boosts microphone input before processing. This can help a quiet mic, but it can also amplify room noise, breathing, keyboard sounds, and clipping.
audioProcessing.vadHelpTitle: Tell me more about voice detection sensitivity
audioProcessing.vadDescription: VAD determines when you're speaking vs when there's silence or background noise. The threshold controls how confident the system must be before recording audio.
audioProcessing.lowThresholdDesc: Very sensitive — captures quiet speech but may include noise. Good for quiet environments.
audioProcessing.midThresholdDesc: Balanced — reduces background noise while still capturing most speech.
audioProcessing.highThresholdDesc: Conservative — only passes clear speech. Reduces stuttering artifacts but may miss quiet words.
audioProcessing.vadTip: If you're experiencing stuttering artifacts like 'wh wh wh why', try increasing this value to 0.5 or 0.6. The trade-off is that very quiet speech might get clipped.

fuzzyMatching.title: Fuzzy Matching Settings
fuzzyMatching.toggleDesc: Handle typos, mishearings, and similar-sounding words
fuzzyMatching.description: Fuzzy matching helps recognize voice commands even with slight variations, typos, or pronunciation differences.
fuzzyMatching.levenshteinDesc: Handles typos and misheard letters. For example, 'srart' matches 'start' because only one letter is different.
fuzzyMatching.phoneticDesc: Matches words that sound similar. For example, 'edge' and 'etch' have the same phonetic code.
fuzzyMatching.tip: Tip: If commands aren't matching, try lowering the thresholds. If too many false matches occur, raise them.

transcriptionSystemPrompt.title: Voice Model Prompt (optional, will only work if STT model supports it)
transcriptionSystemPrompt.description: Provide context to help the voice model with specialized terminology, names, or formatting.
transcriptionSystemPrompt.tooltip: This prompt provides context hints directly to the Speech-to-Text (STT) model to help it recognize specialized terminology, proper nouns, or formatting before the transcript is created. Unlike LLM Post-Processing, this works at the 'hearing' stage to prevent errors. Note that prompts are saved independently for each specific voice model—switching models will switch the active prompt. Because STT engines have small memory windows for context (often 224-448 tokens), if the input exceeds this limit, it will be automatically truncated from the start. Support depends on the specific model provider (e.g., Whisper models generally support this, while Parakeet does not).
transcriptionSystemPrompt.placeholder: Example: Context: technical discussion. Terms: Kubernetes, gRPC, CI/CD.

liveSoundTranscription.title: Live Monitor
liveSoundTranscription.description: Capture audio from your speakers, microphone, or both and read the live transcript right here.

whatIsThis.content:
Live Monitor streams audio from your speakers, microphone, or both into a real-time transcript on this page — with optional speaker identification so you can see who said what.

Unlike dictation, Live Monitor never inserts text into other apps. Use it to follow meetings, calls, or any audio, then review, process with AI, or save the result to a file.

Tip: real-time streaming from microphone can behave differently than from speakers — if results feel slow or cut off, try adjusting the session settings below.

session.title: Session
session.description: Start and stop a dedicated live transcription workflow without auto-pasting into another app.
session.remoteOnly: Select Soniox or Deepgram as provider above to use this page as a realtime session surface.
session.anotherSessionActive: Another preview-owned transcription session is active. Stop it before using this page.
session.process: Send to LLM for post processing
session.diarizationDisabledHint: Live speaker separation is off for this page. Turn it on here if you want diarized live transcript blocks.
session.diarizationToggleDescription: This toggle only affects Live Monitor on this page. It does not change normal speech / mic transcription.
session.recordingHint: Live transcription is active. Finalized text stays here immediately as it arrives.
session.idleHint: Start a live session to stream transcript updates directly into this page.

transcript.title: Transcript
transcript.description: Interim and finalized transcript output stays here as the session runs, including speaker-separated live segments when diarization is available.
transcript.previewDescription: Speaker-aware transcript stream for this page-owned live session.
transcript.empty: Live transcript output will appear here.
transcript.interimActive: Interim speech is still being refined.

sessionOverrides.endpointDetectionHint: When off, the provider never auto-finalizes mid-session — useful if you experience long gaps after a phrase ends.
sessionOverrides.maxEndpointDelayHint: How long the provider waits after silence before finalizing a segment. Lower = faster finalization, but may cut off speech.
sessionOverrides.endpointingMsHint: Milliseconds of silence before Deepgram finalizes a segment. Lower = faster, but may cut speech short.

speakerNames.title: Speaker Names
speakerNames.hint: Rename speakers as they appear — names update the transcript live.
speakerNames.noSpeakers: No speakers detected yet. Start transcription to see speakers here.

boost.title: Microphone Input Boost
boost.description: Saved separately for each microphone device.
boost.warning: May help a quiet mic, but it can also amplify room noise, breathing, keyboard sounds, and clipping.

autoSelect.label: Auto-select by microphone name mask
autoSelect.description: Checked only right before recording starts. If auto-select is enabled and the mask is not empty, AivoRelay first tries the first microphone whose name matches the mask. If nothing matches, it falls back to your previous manual microphone if available, otherwise Default. No background polling or watcher is used.

autoSelectMask.title: Microphone Name Mask
autoSelectMask.description: Mask rules: * matches any number of characters, ? matches exactly one character, and without wildcards the text matches any part of the microphone name case-insensitively. Examples: Yeti* matches Yeti Stereo Microphone; Wireless ? matches Wireless A but not Wireless Headset; usb matches any microphone name containing usb.

audioFeedback.label: Audio Feedback
audioFeedback.description: Play sound when recording starts and stops

outputDevice.title: Output Device
outputDevice.description: Select your preferred audio output device for feedback sounds

volume.title: Volume
volume.description: Adjust the volume of audio feedback sounds

autoStop.title: Recording Auto-Stop Safety
autoStop.description: Automatically stops recording if it's left on for too long. Crucial safety feature to prevent draining your cloud API budget if you accidentally forget to stop recording.
autoStop.timeoutDescription: Time in seconds before an active recording is automatically stopped.
autoStop.pasteDescription: If disabled, the transcription will be canceled and no text will be pasted when auto-stopped.

tellMeMore.title: Tell me more: Fine-tuning the Engine
tellMeMore.headline: Fine-tune the Engine.
tellMeMore.intro: These settings control system behavior and how AivoRelay interacts with your applications.

startup.title: Startup Options:
startup.description: Control whether AivoRelay launches when you turn on your PC and whether it starts minimized to the system tray.

pasteMethod.title: Paste Method:
pasteMethod.description: How transcribed text gets into your apps.
pasteMethod.clipboard: Clipboard: Pastes instantly using Ctrl+V (faster, works in most apps).
pasteMethod.direct: Direct: Simulates typing character-by-character (more compatible with games/terminals).

clipboardHandling.title: Clipboard Handling:
clipboardHandling.intro: This setting decides what happens to whatever is already in your clipboard after AivoRelay pastes the transcript.
clipboardHandling.safestChoiceDescription: If you are not sure which one to choose, use Restore previous text. It is the safest everyday option for most people.

dontModify.title: Restore previous text:
dontModify.description: AivoRelay remembers the text currently in your clipboard, pastes the transcript, then tries to put your old text back. Best if you often copy notes, links, or code and do not want the transcript to replace them. This usually restores plain text only.

copyToClipboard.title: Keep transcription in clipboard:
copyToClipboard.description: AivoRelay leaves the transcript in your clipboard after pasting. Best if you want to paste the same transcript again in another app. Your previous clipboard content will be replaced by the transcript.

restoreAdvanced.title: Restore all formats (Windows only, experimental):
restoreAdvanced.description: This mode tries to back up and restore more than plain text, including copied images, files, and formatted content. Use it only if the normal restore option is not enough. It can be less predictable because different apps expose clipboard data in different ways.

clipboardHandling.note: If clipboard behavior ever feels unreliable, switch back to Restore previous text first.

modelUnload.title: Model Unload:
modelUnload.description: Automatically free GPU/CPU memory when the speech model hasn't been used for a while. Useful if you need resources for other apps.

whisper.title: Whisper Acceleration
whisper.description: Hardware acceleration for Whisper models. Auto uses GPU if available (Metal on macOS, Vulkan on Windows/Linux).

ort.title: ONNX Acceleration
ort.description: Hardware acceleration for ONNX models (Parakeet, Canary, Moonshine, etc.). DirectML on Windows is experimental. Models may fail to transcribe.

acceleration.note: If you are unsure, leave both settings on Auto. Changing acceleration unloads the current local model so the new backend is used the next time it loads.

customWords.title: Custom Words:
customWords.description: Configure this in Text Processing > Fuzzy Word Correction. Add words that are often misheard (names, technical terms, brand names) so similar-sounding words are corrected automatically.

tellMeMore.tip: Most users can leave these at their defaults. Adjust only if you experience specific issues.

rememberSize.label: Remember window size
rememberSize.description: Restore the window to its previous size when the app starts.

rememberPosition.label: Remember window position
rememberPosition.description: Restore the window to its previous position on screen when the app starts.

startHidden.label: Start Hidden
startHidden.description: Launch to system tray without opening the window.

autostart.label: Launch on Startup
autostart.description: Automatically start AivoRelay when you log in to your computer.

showTrayIcon.label: Show Tray Icon
showTrayIcon.description: Display the AivoRelay icon in the system tray.

overlay.title: Overlay Position
overlay.description: Display visual feedback overlay during recording and transcription. On Linux 'None' is recommended.

errorDuration.title: Error Overlay Duration
errorDuration.description: How long error overlays stay visible before auto-hide.

errorVisibility.label: Show Error Overlay
errorVisibility.description: Show runtime errors in the recording overlay.
errorVisibility.danger: Dangerous: disabling overlay errors can hide broken setup and failed transcriptions.

allowReservedAreas.label: Allow Auto-Positioning in Reserved Areas
allowReservedAreas.description: Let auto-placed overlay, preview, confirmation, and voice button windows use taskbar or docked-bar space. Manual dragging can still place them anywhere.

dragGrip.label: Show Recording Overlay Drag Grip
dragGrip.description: Show a small top handle on hover so you can drag the recording overlay during a session without leaving the grip visible all the time.
dragGrip.help: Tip: move the pointer over the overlay to reveal the grip, then drag from the top center.

pasteMethod.title: Paste Method
pasteMethod.description: Choose how text is inserted. Direct: simulates typing via system input. None: skips paste, only updates history/clipboard.

convertLfToCrlf.label: Convert LF to CRLF (Windows)
convertLfToCrlf.description: Normalize Unix-style line breaks (LF) to Windows-style (CRLF) when pasting via clipboard. Fixes newlines being 'eaten' in some applications.

pasteDelay.title: Paste Delay
pasteDelay.description: Delay before sending paste keystroke (in milliseconds). Increase if wrong text is being pasted.

tellMeMore.title: Tell me more: Paste Methods Explained
tellMeMore.headline: Understanding Paste Methods
tellMeMore.intro: Different applications handle text input differently. Choose the method that works best for your workflow:

ctrlV.title: Clipboard (Ctrl+V):
ctrlV.description: Uses 'tauri-plugin-clipboard-manager' to write plain text (CF_UNICODETEXT on Windows, text/plain on Linux/macOS) to the OS clipboard, then uses 'Enigo' to simulate the paste shortcut. Handles large text instantly. Note: Clipboard backup/restore only supports text—if you had an image or rich content copied, it will be replaced with the transcription text.

direct.title: Direct:
direct.description: Simulates typing each character individually using the Rust 'Enigo' library. This happens at the OS input level. Slower but more compatible with games, terminals, and legacy apps that block clipboard paste. Newlines work reliably as explicit Enter keypresses.

none.title: None:
none.description: Skips the paste step entirely. Useful if you only want to save the transcription to history or manually copy it later. The text is processed and stored, but no input simulation occurs.

pasteDelay.title: Paste Delay:
pasteDelay.description: Some applications read the clipboard too quickly and may paste older content. This delay gives the OS a moment to commit the new text before the paste keystroke is sent.

crlfNote.title: Windows Line Endings:
crlfNote.description: LLM responses and some APIs use Unix-style line breaks (LF). Windows applications often expect Windows-style line breaks (CRLF). When enabled, this option converts LF to CRLF before pasting, preventing newlines from being ignored or collapsed.

tellMeMore.tip: Most users should use Clipboard (Ctrl+V). Try Direct if you experience issues with games or terminals.

clipboardHandling.title: Clipboard Handling
clipboardHandling.description: Choose what happens to your clipboard after pasting. If you are not sure, use Restore previous text.
clipboardHandling.descriptionAdvanced: Experimental Windows mode. Tries to restore text, images, files, and other clipboard formats after pasting. Use it only if you specifically need better recovery of rich clipboard content.

autoSubmit.title: Auto Submit
autoSubmit.description: Automatically send the selected key combination after text insertion. Cmd+Enter applies on macOS, while Windows/Linux use Super+Enter.

allowNoSelection.label: Allow Without Selection
allowNoSelection.description: If no text is selected, generate new text based on the instruction.

noSelectionSystemPrompt.title: System Prompt (No Selection)
noSelectionSystemPrompt.description: System prompt used when generating text without a selection.

systemPrompt.title: AI Replace System Prompt
systemPrompt.description: System prompt that enforces paste-ready output for AI Replace Selection.

userPrompt.title: AI Replace User Prompt
userPrompt.description: Template for the AI Replace request. Supports ${instruction} and ${output}.

maxChars.title: AI Replace Max Characters
maxChars.description: Maximum number of characters allowed in the selected text before calling the LLM.

allowQuickTap.label: Allow Quick Tap
allowQuickTap.description: Enable to send without speaking. Usage: Quickly tap your hotkey and do not say anything to automatically improve/fix the selected text.

quickTapSystemPrompt.title: System Prompt (Quick Tap)
quickTapSystemPrompt.description: System prompt used when you just tap the key without speaking (e.g. 'Fix grammar').

quickTapThreshold.title: Quick Tap Threshold
quickTapThreshold.description: Maximum duration to consider a tap as a 'Quick Tap'.

transcriptionProvider.title: Transcription Provider
transcriptionProvider.description: Select the engine used for speech-to-text conversion.

options.local: Local (Offline, Privacy-focused, Needs powerful GPU, drivers and Vulkan softaware)

providerPreset.title: Remote Provider Preset
providerPreset.description: Use locked HTTPS presets for Groq or OpenAI. Switch to Custom only when you need a self-hosted or non-standard endpoint.

baseUrl.title: Remote Base URL
baseUrl.description: Known providers use locked HTTPS URLs. Only the Custom preset can edit this field.

customHttpOverride.title: Allow Insecure HTTP
customHttpOverride.description: Advanced override for custom/local endpoints only. Leave this OFF unless you intentionally trust a local or self-hosted plain HTTP server.
customHttpOverride.warning: Warning: plain HTTP can expose your API key and transcript contents to interception or tampering. Use it only for trusted local/self-hosted testing.

modelId.title: Remote Model ID
modelId.description: Model identifier to send in the transcription request.

apiKey.title: Remote API Key
apiKey.description: Stored securely in Windows Credential Manager.
apiKey.statusStoredHint: Stored in Windows Credential Manager and available to AivoRelay when needed. The key isn't shown here.
apiKey.replaceHint: Enter a new key to replace the stored one.

capture.title: Debug Capture
capture.description: Capture remote STT debug logs in memory.

mode.title: Debug Mode
mode.description: Normal captures errors only; verbose includes request metadata.

reset.title: Soniox Quick Actions
reset.description: Reset all Soniox settings in this section to recommended defaults.
reset.success: Soniox settings were reset to defaults.

tellMeMore.title: Tell me more: Soniox Real-time
tellMeMore.headline: How Soniox real-time behaves in AivoRelay.
tellMeMore.liveFlow: When Live mode is enabled, microphone audio is streamed directly to Soniox over WebSocket. Soniox can emit provisional and final tokens, but AivoRelay focuses on stable final output so your typed text does not flicker.
tellMeMore.stopFlow: On Stop, AivoRelay immediately stops microphone capture in the UI, then sends Soniox finalization, waits up to your configured timeout for late final tokens, and finally closes the live session. In practice: recording feels stopped instantly, while the app may still collect the last 1-2 words in the background.

realtimeBehavior.title: Real-time behavior from Soniox docs (as applied in AivoRelay)
realtimeBehavior.tokenDraft: Non-final tokens (is_final=false) are provisional and may change; final tokens (is_final=true) are stable and will not change afterward.
realtimeBehavior.tokenFinal: AivoRelay UX goal is stable inserted text while speaking, with minimal tail loss when you stop.
realtimeBehavior.manualFinalize: On Stop, AivoRelay sends {"type":"finalize"} to ask Soniox to finalize pending audio before session shutdown.
realtimeBehavior.finMarker: Soniox may emit a <fin> token after manual finalization; this is a control marker, not user text to type.
realtimeBehavior.keepalive: During silence, keepalive messages should be sent regularly to avoid connection timeout (Soniox guidance: at least once every 20s).
realtimeBehavior.silenceRule: For best accuracy/latency balance, finalize after a short silence (~200ms) at end of speech, not too early.

userStory.title: User story in this app
userStory.item1: As a user, I press one shortcut, speak naturally, and see stable text appear while I am still talking.
userStory.item2: When I press Stop, recording ends immediately in UI, while AivoRelay finalizes remaining words in background.
userStory.item3: I can tune speed vs completeness: faster stop for responsiveness, or longer finalization for fewer cut tail words.

matrix.title: When to use / Tradeoff / Recommended value

live.title: Live Real-time Streaming
live.whenToUse: You want words to appear while speaking and minimum interaction latency.
live.tradeoff: More sensitive to network stability than async mode.
live.recommended: ON for stt-rt models; OFF only if you explicitly prefer non-live flow.

finalizeTimeout.title: Live Finalization Timeout
finalizeTimeout.whenToUse: Use this when the transcript sometimes cuts the ending after you press Stop, or when your network is inconsistent and final tokens arrive late.
finalizeTimeout.tradeoff: Higher values improve completeness (fewer missing tail words) but keep the session alive longer in the background before full completion. Lower values feel faster but increase the chance of dropping the very end of speech.
finalizeTimeout.recommended: Default is 500ms for fast stop. Increase if tail words are often cut; raise further only on unstable networks.

instantStop.title: Instant Stop (AivoRelay local feature)
instantStop.whenToUse: You need immediate stop feedback and can accept occasional tail truncation.
instantStop.tradeoff: Late final chunks are ignored; last words can be cut.
instantStop.recommended: OFF by default; enable only for ultra-fast interaction workflows.

languageHints.title: Language Hints
languageHints.whenToUse: You mostly speak known language(s) and want fewer misdetections.
languageHints.tradeoff: Wrong hints can bias recognition away from actual speech.
languageHints.recommended: Use one primary language code first (for example: en).

endpointDetection.title: Endpoint Detection
endpointDetection.whenToUse: You want automatic utterance finalization without manual timing.
endpointDetection.tradeoff: Very aggressive endpoint settings may finalize slightly too early in edge cases.
endpointDetection.recommended: ON for most users; keep max endpoint delay around 2000ms as baseline.

parameters.model: Model: choose stt-rt-* for real-time streaming or stt-async-* for async pipeline.
parameters.live: Live Real-time Streaming: enables direct WebSocket streaming path for stt-rt models.
parameters.timeout: Soniox Timeout: timeout for non-live/legacy Soniox requests.
parameters.finalizeTimeout: Live Finalization Timeout: after you press Stop, AivoRelay waits this many milliseconds for Soniox to return remaining final tokens. During this window, recording is already stopped in UI, but trailing words can still be appended if they arrive. When timeout is reached, session is force-closed.
parameters.instantStop: Instant Stop (AivoRelay feature): ends live session immediately on Stop and ignores late chunks; fastest UX but highest risk of cutting last words.
parameters.languageHints: Language Hints: enter comma-separated codes in the field (for example: en, es). Do not type JSON in the UI input; JSON docs show only API payload shape.
parameters.profileLanguageHintOnly: Use Current Profile Language As Hint: ignores manual Language Hints and sends exactly one hint derived from the active profile language (or global language if no profile is active).
parameters.strict: Soniox strict Language Restriction: strongly constrains output to language hints.
parameters.endpoint: Endpoint Detection: Soniox semantic end-of-utterance detection.
parameters.keepalive: Keepalive Interval: ping period during silence to keep WebSocket alive (recommended 5-10s, should never exceed 20s idle).
parameters.languageIdentification: Language Identification: includes per-token detected language metadata.
parameters.speakerDiarization: Speaker Diarization: includes per-token speaker label metadata.

tellMeMore.tip: Recommended baseline: stt-rt-v4, Live ON, endpoint detection ON, keepalive 10s, finalize timeout 500ms (increase only if needed). Default settings are good. Just try to input your API key and try them out.

model.title: Soniox Model
model.description: Model identifier sent to Soniox on each request. In AivoRelay, stt-rt-* with Live ON uses the live WebSocket path where text appears while you speak; stt-async-* (or Live OFF) uses non-live processing where text appears after processing finishes. If behavior feels wrong, model family and Live toggle are the first things to verify.

timeout.title: Soniox Timeout (seconds)
timeout.description: Overall timeout for Soniox non-live requests and async file operations (10-300s). In AivoRelay UX, higher values reduce premature failures on slow networks but can make you wait longer before an error is shown; lower values fail faster but are more sensitive to temporary latency. Mode scope: this setting does not control active Live streaming session timing.

live.title: Live Real-time Streaming
live.description: When enabled with stt-rt models, AivoRelay streams microphone audio directly to Soniox over WebSocket and inserts confirmed tokens while you are speaking. This gives the fastest interactive typing experience, but it also depends more on connection stability than async mode.
live.realtimeOnly: Live streaming is only available for real-time Soniox models (stt-rt-*).

languageHints.title: Language Hints
languageHints.description: Type language codes as a comma-separated list in this input field (for example: en, es or en, es, ru). Do not type JSON here. API equivalent for two languages is: { "language_hints": ["en", "es"] }. Soniox is biased toward these languages, which usually improves recognition accuracy and reduces wrong-language guesses. Mode scope: applied in Live mode and used as defaults for Soniox File Transcription; not currently used as-is by the non-live shortcut fallback path, which derives hint from current profile/global language.

profileLanguageHintOnly.title: Use Current Profile Language As Hint
profileLanguageHintOnly.description: When enabled, AivoRelay ignores the Language Hints list and sends exactly one hint derived from the active profile language (or global language if no profile is active). This keeps Soniox language bias aligned with the profile and avoids stale manual hints. Mode scope: Live mode behavior; this setting does not reconfigure Soniox async file options or non-live shortcut fallback behavior.

languageHintsStrict.title: Soniox Strict Language Restriction
languageHintsStrict.titleTooltip: This option only appears if a Soniox model is used
languageHintsStrict.description: Recommended usage: use a single language whenever possible. Language restriction is most robust when only one language is provided. Example: { "language_hints": ["en"], "language_hints_strict": true }. Multiple languages reduce robustness: you can provide more than one language, but accuracy may degrade when language identification becomes ambiguous (for example heavy accents or acoustically similar languages). Example: { "language_hints": ["en", "es"], "language_hints_strict": true }. In difficult cases, Soniox may still choose a wrong language/script, so single-language strict mode is strongly recommended when correctness is critical. Mode scope: currently Live mode only; this flag is not sent in non-live shortcut fallback or Soniox async file requests.

endpointDetection.title: Endpoint Detection
endpointDetection.description: Lets Soniox detect natural end-of-utterance moments and finalize tokens automatically (emits <end>). In AivoRelay this improves perceived responsiveness because final words stabilize sooner, especially during conversational pauses. Mode scope: intended as Live tuning; in current non-live shortcut fallback this is forced ON internally, and Soniox async file API does not expose this control.

maxEndpointDelay.title: Max Endpoint Delay (ms)
maxEndpointDelay.description: Upper bound for endpoint latency after speech stops (500-3000, default 2000). Lower values feel snappier in AivoRelay but may finalize slightly earlier; higher values wait longer, which can preserve accuracy when speech cadence is irregular. Mode scope: Live mode only; not applied in non-live shortcut fallback or Soniox async file transcription.

keepalive.title: Keepalive Interval (seconds)
keepalive.description: How often keepalive is sent during silence (5-20s). Soniox guidance: send at least once every 20 seconds whenever audio is not being sent. In AivoRelay this keeps the same live session alive between pauses, preserves session context, and reduces reconnect-related instability. Mode scope: Live WebSocket sessions only.

finalizeTimeout.title: Live Finalization Timeout (ms)
finalizeTimeout.description: What you see: when you press Stop, recording ends immediately in UI. What happens next: AivoRelay still waits in background for late Soniox final tokens, up to this timeout, then force-closes the session (100-20000ms). Default is 500ms. Increase this value if last words are often missing; decrease it only if you want even more aggressive completion and can accept occasional tail truncation. If you want almost instant finish, use Instant Stop. Mode scope: Live mode only.

instantStop.title: Instant Stop (Local Feature)
instantStop.description: AivoRelay-only behavior: on Stop, immediately cancels Soniox live session and ignores late chunks. UX becomes very snappy, but final trailing words are more likely to be cut when network or server finalization is delayed. Mode scope: Live mode only.

languageIdentification.title: Language Identification
languageIdentification.description: Requests per-token language metadata from Soniox. In AivoRelay this mostly affects metadata/diagnostics and multilingual analysis flows; it does not directly change normal plain-text insertion behavior. Mode scope: sent in Live mode and Soniox async file jobs; currently not sent in non-live shortcut fallback WebSocket path.

speakerDiarization.title: Speaker Diarization
speakerDiarization.description: Requests speaker labels in token metadata. In AivoRelay this is mainly useful for multi-speaker scenarios and downstream analysis; regular typing output remains clean text unless speaker labels are explicitly used in a specific flow. Mode scope: sent in Live mode and Soniox async file jobs; currently not sent in non-live shortcut fallback WebSocket path.

apiKey.title: Soniox API Key
apiKey.description: Stored securely in Windows Credential Manager.

deepgram.banner: Deepgram live mode uses WebSocket streaming with KeepAlive / Finalize / CloseStream control messages. KeepAlive is recommended every 3-5 seconds during silence.

reset.title: Deepgram Quick Actions
reset.description: Reset all Deepgram settings in this section to recommended defaults (model: nova-3, keepalive: 5s, endpointing: 400ms, finalize timeout: 1200ms).
reset.success: Deepgram settings were reset to defaults.

tellMeMore.title: Tell me more: Deepgram Live Streaming
tellMeMore.headline: How Deepgram streaming behaves in AivoRelay.
tellMeMore.liveFlow: When Live mode is enabled, AivoRelay opens a persistent Deepgram WebSocket (`wss://api.deepgram.com/v1/listen`) and streams 16kHz mono linear16 audio. Final chunks are appended to output while interim text is shown in preview.
tellMeMore.stopFlow: On Stop, AivoRelay ends microphone capture in UI immediately, then sends Deepgram Finalize and CloseStream, waits up to your configured Live Finalization Timeout, and closes the session. This keeps stop interaction responsive while still collecting late final words.

controlMessages.title: Control messages from Deepgram docs (as used by this app)
controlMessages.keepalive: Send {"type":"KeepAlive"} during silence. Deepgram recommends every 3-5 seconds to reduce idle disconnect risk.
controlMessages.finalize: Send {"type":"Finalize"} to force processing of buffered audio and emit final results immediately.
controlMessages.closeStream: Send {"type":"CloseStream"} to request graceful shutdown: process cached data, send final metadata, then close WebSocket.
controlMessages.fromFinalize: Results emitted because of Finalize may include `from_finalize: true`, indicating they were flushed during explicit finalization.
controlMessages.metadata: Session is considered complete when Metadata arrives (or socket closes after graceful shutdown).
controlMessages.netTimeout: Troubleshooting docs: if no audio or text frame reaches Deepgram for ~10 seconds, the connection can close with NET-0001.

transcriptTiming.title: Transcript timing and chunk stability
transcriptTiming.interimVsFinal: With `interim_results=true`, Deepgram sends evolving hypotheses (`is_final=false`) that may change before finalization.
transcriptTiming.speechFinal: With endpointing enabled, Deepgram can mark utterance boundaries using `speech_final=true`. Current app insertion logic remains based on `is_final` chunks.
transcriptTiming.concatRule: AivoRelay appends only final chunks into committed output and keeps interim text preview-only.

matrix.title: When to use / Tradeoff / Recommended value

model.title: Model
model.whenToUse: Choose model by domain: general dictation vs healthcare-specific vocabulary.
model.tradeoff: Domain-specific models can be better for niche terminology but may be less optimal outside that domain.
model.recommended: Start with `nova-3`; switch to `nova-3-medical` only for medical dictation.

live.title: Live Streaming
live.whenToUse: You need words to appear during speech with lowest interaction latency.
live.tradeoff: Live sessions are more sensitive to network quality than non-live one-shot flow.
live.recommended: ON for interactive dictation; OFF only if you explicitly want non-live behavior.

interim.title: Interim Results
interim.whenToUse: You want continuous visual feedback while speaking.
interim.tradeoff: Interim hypotheses can flicker/change until final chunks arrive.
interim.recommended: ON for live preview UX; OFF for stricter final-only display.

endpointing.title: Endpointing
endpointing.whenToUse: You want automatic phrase boundary detection around natural pauses.
endpointing.tradeoff: Aggressive endpointing can split speech too early in some speaking styles.

endpointingMs.title: Endpointing (ms)
endpointingMs.whenToUse: Tune pause sensitivity for your speaking style and use case.
endpointingMs.tradeoff: Lower values react faster but can cut phrases earlier; higher values are safer but slower to finalize.
endpointingMs.recommended: Default 400ms in app. Deepgram guidance: around 300-500ms for conversational agents; very fast bots may use lower values.

keepalive.title: KeepAlive Interval
keepalive.whenToUse: Your sessions include natural pauses/silence and you want stable long-lived streaming.
keepalive.tradeoff: Too infrequent keepalive increases idle timeout risk; too frequent increases control traffic (small overhead).

finalizeTimeout.title: Live Finalization Timeout
finalizeTimeout.whenToUse: Increase if trailing words are occasionally missing after Stop.
finalizeTimeout.tradeoff: Higher value improves tail completeness but keeps session finalization active longer in background.
finalizeTimeout.recommended: 1200ms default. Raise only if needed for late final chunks.

instantStop.title: Instant Stop (AivoRelay local feature)
instantStop.whenToUse: You prioritize immediate stop/cancel UX and can tolerate occasional tail loss.
instantStop.tradeoff: Skips waiting for late final chunks, so the end of speech can be truncated.
instantStop.recommended: OFF by default; enable only for ultra-fast interaction workflows.

parameters.model: Model: sent as `model=<value>` in streaming query params. App default is `nova-3`.
parameters.timeout: Deepgram Timeout: non-live timeout (10-3600s) for one-shot and file transcription operations.
parameters.live: Live Streaming: enables persistent WebSocket capture path while recording.
parameters.interim: Interim Results: maps to `interim_results` query param. Deepgram API default is false; app default is true.
parameters.smartFormat: Smart Format: maps to `smart_format` query param. Improves punctuation/number readability. API default is false; app default is true.
parameters.endpointing: Endpointing: enables/disables Deepgram endpointing behavior (query param `endpointing`).
parameters.endpointingMs: Endpointing (ms): when endpointing is enabled, app sends numeric `endpointing=<ms>` (allowed 50-5000 in app; default 400).
parameters.keepalive: KeepAlive Interval: app sends `{"type":"KeepAlive"}` during silence; value is clamped to 3-5s.
parameters.finalizeTimeout: Live Finalization Timeout: after Stop, app sends Finalize + CloseStream and waits this many milliseconds for late final chunks before force-completing.
parameters.instantStop: Instant Stop: local app shortcut that cancels live session immediately and skips normal finalization wait.
parameters.apiKey: API Key: stored in Windows Credential Manager and sent as `Authorization: Token <key>` for Deepgram requests.

docs.title: Official Deepgram documentation links:
docs.liveApi: Streaming API Reference (`/v1/listen` query params and message schema)
docs.keepalive: Audio Keep Alive (recommended cadence and payload)

tellMeMore.tip: Recommended baseline in this app: `nova-3`, Live ON, Interim ON, Smart Format ON, Endpointing ON (400ms), KeepAlive 5s, Finalization Timeout 1200ms, Instant Stop OFF.

model.title: Deepgram Model
model.description: Model identifier passed to Deepgram on each request (`model=<value>`). For most dictation use `nova-3`; use `nova-3-medical` only when your speech is medical-domain heavy. The app keeps this value customizable so you can adopt newer Deepgram models without waiting for UI updates.

timeout.title: Deepgram Timeout (seconds)
timeout.description: Overall timeout for Deepgram non-live and file-transcription operations (10-3600s). Higher values are more tolerant of transient latency but delay failure reporting; lower values fail faster but are less tolerant to slow networks. This is not the same as live-session finalization timeout.

live.title: Live Streaming
live.description: When enabled, recording uses Deepgram WebSocket live flow and can emit transcript updates while you speak. On Stop, app finalizes the stream in background (Finalize + CloseStream) and then closes session.

interim.title: Interim Results
interim.description: Enables evolving hypotheses before chunks become final. Deepgram marks these as `is_final=false`; they may change until finalization. In this app interim text is preview-only, while committed output is appended from final chunks.

smartFormat.title: Smart Format
smartFormat.description: Requests Deepgram smart formatting for punctuation, capitalization, numbers, dates, and related readability improvements. Useful for immediate paste-ready text with less manual cleanup.

endpointing.title: Endpointing
endpointing.description: Enables Deepgram endpointing to detect utterance boundaries from pauses. Deepgram can emit `speech_final=true` when a natural endpoint is detected. In current app logic, committed insertion still follows final chunks (`is_final=true`).

endpointingMs.title: Endpointing (ms)
endpointingMs.description: Pause sensitivity value for endpointing. Lower values react faster but can split speech earlier; higher values are safer but slower to finalize. App range is 50-5000ms, default 400ms. Deepgram guidance generally suggests around 300-500ms for natural conversational behavior.

keepalive.title: KeepAlive Interval (seconds)
keepalive.description: Interval for sending `{"type":"KeepAlive"}` text messages during silence. Deepgram recommends 3-5 seconds for live streaming sessions to reduce idle timeout risk (NET-0001 around ~10s of inactivity). App enforces this same 3-5s range.

finalizeTimeout.title: Live Finalization Timeout (ms)

tellMeMore.title: Tell me more: Shortcut Engines
tellMeMore.intro: AivoRelay offers three different methods (Engines) for detecting when you press your recording hotkeys. Each has different trade-offs in compatibility, performance, and security.

tauri.title: Native Engine (Tauri / RegisterHotKey)
tauri.description: The recommended engine for most users; its limitations are hardcoded into the win32 RegisterHotKey API because Windows prioritizes system stability over individual app flexibility. Technically, the OS kernel takes over the keyboard monitoring; AivoRelay remains completely idle until the system posts a WM_HOTKEY message directly to its internal message queue. This architecture provides near-zero overhead because there is no continuous polling and zero risk of system-wide input lag.

pros.title: Pros:
pros.minimalOverhead: Minimal overhead: Windows only wakes AivoRelay when the exact shortcut is pressed.
pros.security: Security: Uses official high-level system calls, making it much less likely to be flagged by antivirus or anti-cheat software.

cons.title: Limitations:
cons.lockKeys: Cannot use 'Lock' keys (Caps Lock, Num Lock) as modifiers (though they can be the main key).
cons.conflicts: Shortcut registration may fail if another application is already using it.

handyKeys.title: Upstream Backend (HandyKeys)
handyKeys.description: This engine ports Handy's upstream handy-keys backend into AivoRelay. It keeps shortcut handling in a dedicated backend manager thread and also provides backend-side shortcut recording, which is useful for keys the browser cannot capture reliably.

pros.title: Pros:
pros.compatibility: Better shortcut coverage than Tauri without replacing the fork's existing rdev monitor-only features.
pros.recording: Can record shortcuts through the backend instead of relying only on browser keyboard events.

cons.title: Limitations:
cons.restart: Like the other Windows engine changes in AivoRelay, switching to HandyKeys still requires an app restart.
cons.scope: Passive key-monitor features in the fork still use rdev where that behavior is required.

rdev.title: Hook Engine (rdev / SetWindowsHookEx)
rdev.description: A low-level engine providing unrestricted access by utilizing a WH_KEYBOARD_LL global hook. This mechanism injects a callback procedure into the Windows input processing stream, forcing the OS to pass every single keyboard event system-wide to AivoRelay for inspection before it reaches the active application. While this allows for complex shortcuts (like modifier-only or Lock keys), it is the same mechanism used by keyloggers.

pros.title: Pros:
pros.flexibility: Maximum flexibility: Supports any key combination, including 'modifier-only' shortcuts.
pros.enhanced: Enhanced support: Can detect 'Lock' keys and even mouse buttons.

cons.title: Limitations & Risks:
cons.security: Security suspicion: Because it monitors all keystrokes, it may be flagged as 'keylogger-like' by security software.
cons.antiCheat: Anti-Cheat risk: Aggressive anti-cheat (like Vanguard or EAC) may block or flag software using global hooks. Use at your own risk when playing online games with anti-cheat protection.
cons.performance: Performance: Slightly higher CPU usage as every keystroke must be processed. If the app hangs, it could cause brief input lag.
cons.harshReality: Harsh Reality: Even extremely well-established software like AutoHotkey has caused permanent, unappealable bans in games like Apex Legends simply for being active in the background. Anti-cheat systems often monitor the presence of hooks rather than their intent.

verdict.title: Recommendation:
verdict.text: Use Native Engine (Tauri) by default for best performance and security. Switch to Hook Engine (rdev) only if you need a specific shortcut that Tauri doesn't support. Tip: Your mouse or keyboard software can likely remap additional buttons to complex combinations that are inconvenient to use manually, allowing you to use them with the Native Engine.

help.title: What is Browser Connector?
help.description: Browser Connector sends your voice transcriptions directly to AI chat interfaces (like ChatGPT, Claude) through the <link>AivoRelay Connector</link> Chrome extension.
help.feature0: AivoRelay can unpack the extension for you. Click "How to Install the Browser Extension" below and then use "Extract Here".

help.feature4: Security Recommendation: You should change the connection password. On a fresh install, the extension uses a default password that could allow other programs to intercept your communication. Changing it here will automatically update the extension if it is connected. For maximum security, we recommend doing this while offline to prevent any local interference during the handshake.
help.feature5: AivoRelay exposes this connector through a local Axum v{{version}} web server on your computer. If you consider this Axum version unsafe for your threat model, disable Browser Connector entirely.

tellMeMore.title: How to Install the Browser Extension
tellMeMore.headline: Bridge the gap to the Web.
tellMeMore.intro: The AivoRelay Connector Chrome extension connects this desktop app to AI chat tabs in your browser.
tellMeMore.compatibility: This AivoRelay build is currently packaged for AivoRelay Connector v{{version}}.

getExtension.title: Get the Extension:
getExtension.step1: Browse the source on GitHub: <link>AivoRelay Connector</link>
getExtension.step2: Use the export button below for a ready-to-load folder, or download the GitHub zip and extract it yourself.

actions.pathHint: Choose the parent folder, not the final extension folder. AivoRelay will create the "AivoRelay Connector" folder inside it.
actions.reloadHint: If this folder is already loaded as an unpacked extension in Chrome/Edge, open chrome://extensions and click Reload for AivoRelay Connector. Browsers do not auto-reload unpacked extensions when files change on disk.
actions.identityHint: The extension identity is also part of AivoRelay's trusted CORS allow-list for the local connector server. Keeping the same identity preserves that browser trust for this exported copy.
actions.generateNewIdHint: Leave this off to update the connector copy that AivoRelay already exported to this same path. Turn it on only when you intentionally want a new browser-extension identity, new trusted CORS origin, and a new connector pairing state.
actions.generateNewIdForcedHint: Required for this path because AivoRelay has no previous export record here yet. The first export creates a new extension identity, a new trusted CORS origin, and a new initial connector secret.
actions.selectFolderTitle: Choose where to export the browser extension
actions.exportSuccess: Browser extension exported to {{path}}. Configured origin: {{origin}}. A new connector password was generated and saved. The local connector server is refreshing in the background, so the first reconnect can take a few seconds. If Chrome/Edge already has this unpacked folder loaded, click Reload on the extension card to apply the new files.
actions.exportSuccessReusedId: Browser extension exported to {{path}}. Configured origin: {{origin}}. The previous extension ID was kept, so the existing unpacked extension can stay installed. In Chrome/Edge, click Reload on the extension card to apply the updated files.
actions.exportSuccessWithNewId: Browser extension exported to {{path}}. Configured origin: {{origin}}. A fresh extension identity was created for this export. If an older unpacked AivoRelay Connector is still installed in Chrome/Edge, remove it manually and then load this folder again, because the browser will treat it as a different extension.

install.title: Install in Chrome/Edge:
install.step2: Enable "Developer mode" (toggle in the top right)
install.step4: Select the exported or extracted extension folder

setup.step4: Click "Bind to this tab" to connect

extendedThinking.title: Extended Thinking
extendedThinking.description: Enable reasoning tokens for compatible models (e.g., Claude Sonnet on OpenRouter). The model will "think" before responding, which may improve complex tasks but uses more tokens.

budget.title: Reasoning Budget
budget.description: Maximum tokens the model can use for internal reasoning. Minimum 1024. Higher values allow more complex thinking.

chunking.maxMinutesHint: Used by Auto and Custom modes. Default is 0.5 minutes (30 seconds).

textReplacement.title: Text Processing
textReplacement.description: Automatically replace text patterns in transcriptions. Useful for fixing commonly misheard words or applying consistent formatting.
textReplacement.enableDescription: Apply replacement rules to all transcriptions after processing.
textReplacement.outputWhitespaceDescription: Configure how leading/trailing spaces are normalized in final transcription output.
textReplacement.outputWhitespaceLeadingRemoveLabel: Remove leading space if provider returned one
textReplacement.outputWhitespaceLeadingRemoveDescription: If output starts with whitespace, remove it.
textReplacement.outputWhitespaceLeadingAddLabel: Add leading space if provider did not return one
textReplacement.outputWhitespaceLeadingAddDescription: If output starts without whitespace, prefix one space.
textReplacement.outputWhitespaceTrailingRemoveLabel: Remove trailing space if provider returned one
textReplacement.outputWhitespaceTrailingRemoveDescriptio: If output ends with whitespace, remove it.
textReplacement.outputWhitespaceTrailingAddLabel: Add trailing space if provider did not return one
textReplacement.outputWhitespaceTrailingAddDescription: If output ends without whitespace, append one space.
textReplacement.cleanupDescription: Automatically remove common speech artifacts from the final text.
textReplacement.beforeLlmDescription: When enabled, text replacements are applied BEFORE LLM processing. This prevents the LLM from modifying your replacement patterns.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditGroupDescription: Independent feature: monitors your edit key and can lowercase the next suitable transcript chunk even when Text Replacement rules are disabled.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditDescription: Passively monitor a primary key (default: Backspace), with an optional secondary key. After pressing either one, the next suitable transcription chunk can start with lowercase to avoid unwanted sentence capitalization.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditEnableDescription: Use a passive keyboard hook (non-blocking) to detect manual edits and lowercase the next matching chunk.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditKeyDescription: Click the field and press any key or key combination. Monitoring is passive, and any combo containing the monitored key also counts.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditComboHint: If you choose Backspace, Ctrl+Backspace also triggers.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditSecondaryKeyEnableD: Default is OFF. When enabled, pressing either primary or secondary key triggers decapitalize monitoring.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditSecondaryKeyDescrip: Optional second key. Works with OR logic together with the primary key.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditRdevWarningTitle: Warning: this feature uses passive rdev monitoring
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditRdevWarningBody: Only rdev can passively monitor keys like Backspace or Delete while the active application still receives those keys normally.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditRdevWarningTauriBod: Your main Shortcut Engine remains Tauri, but enabling this feature starts an extra rdev listener. Turn this feature off to stop this extra rdev usage.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditRdevWarningNonRdevB: Your main Shortcut Engine is not rdev, but enabling this feature starts an extra rdev listener alongside it. Turn this feature off to stop this extra rdev usage.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditRdevWarningRdevBody: Your main Shortcut Engine is already rdev, so turning this feature off disables only this decapitalize monitor.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditRdevWarningLinkHint: Open Debug -> Experimental Features -> Shortcut Engine and read the rdev warning before using this.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditConflictWarningDesc: Decap monitor may also trigger when these shortcuts are pressed.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditTimeoutDescription: How long (in milliseconds) the decapitalize trigger stays active after pressing a monitored key.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditStandardPostMonitor: After stopping a standard (non-realtime) recording, keep listening for monitored keys for this many milliseconds. 0 disables this extra window.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditTellMeMoreTitle: Tell me more: Decapitalize After Manual Edit
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditStandardModeDesc: after stop-recording in standard STT, AivoRelay can keep monitoring for the configured post-stop window. If you press the monitored key there, the next matching uppercase-start output is decapitalized.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditRealtimeModeDesc: while live chunk typing is running, the next incoming matching chunk that starts uppercase is decapitalized immediately, then the trigger is consumed.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditLanguageNote: If the language/script has no uppercase/lowercase distinction, nothing is changed.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditExample2: You delete the last word with Backspace.
textReplacement.decapitalizeAfterEditExample4: Result becomes: This is night. (not This is Night.)
textReplacement.escapeIntro: Use these codes to match or insert special characters:
textReplacement.escapeUnicode: Unicode character (e.g., \u{200D} for Zero Width Joiner)
textReplacement.caseSensitiveDesc: When enabled, 'Hello' and 'hello' are treated as different. When disabled, both will match.
textReplacement.regexDesc: Enable to use regex patterns for advanced matching. Use $1, $2 in replacement for capture groups.
textReplacement.noteContent: For Windows line endings conversion, consider using the 'Convert LF to CRLF' option in Advanced settings instead — it handles this automatically for clipboard paste operations.
textReplacement.empty: No replacement rules yet. Add one above to get started.
textReplacement.fuzzyWordCorrectionDescription: Add words that are often misheard (names, technical terms). The system will automatically correct similar-sounding words.
textReplacement.fuzzyHowItWorksIntro: This algorithm fixes misheard words by comparing them to your custom list using two methods:
textReplacement.fuzzySoundsLikeDescription: It recognizes that "edge" and "etch" sound similar.
textReplacement.fuzzyLooksLikeDescription: It catches typos like "srart" instead of "start".
textReplacement.fuzzyTip: Tip: If it corrects words too aggressively, lower the sensitivity slider below.
textReplacement.multiWordMatchingDescription: Match up to 3 spoken tokens as one custom term (example: 'Chat G P T' -> 'ChatGPT'). Disable if corrections are too aggressive.
textReplacement.correctionSensitivityDescription: Threshold for fuzzy match score (0.0 = exact match only, 1.0 = accept any). Default 0.18 means a word must be ~82% similar to be corrected.
textReplacement.sonioxRealtimeChunkDescription: Controls how Soniox Live chunk text is corrected before insertion. Default: both options are OFF.
textReplacement.sonioxRealtimeChunkFuzzyLabel: Enable Fuzzy Word Correction for Soniox Live Chunks
textReplacement.sonioxRealtimeChunkFuzzyDescription: Uses typo-tolerant matching from Custom Words on each live chunk. If OFF, chunks skip fuzzy correction but regular Text Replacement rules still run.
textReplacement.sonioxRealtimeChunkSafetyBufferLabel: Keep Safety Buffer for Cross-chunk Matching
textReplacement.sonioxRealtimeChunkSafetyBufferDescripti: Keeps the newest ~3 words briefly so fuzzy correction can match across chunk boundaries. This buffer is used only when fuzzy correction is ON. It delays pasting by about those 3 words, so live output may feel a bit slower (often not noticeable).
textReplacement.sonioxRealtimeChunkBehaviorGuide: For fastest live appearance, keep both OFF. Enable both only when you need better cross-chunk fuzzy correction.
